subtitle: Thursday, 18 June 2026
editor_note: This week's digest highlights a recurring theme: who controls access to AI, and what that means for governments and institutions that depend on it. We look at fresh efforts to bring AI skills to nonprofits and improve safety testing, alongside growing global concerns about reliance on US-based AI systems. For Tamkeen, these stories underline why building local capacity and clear governance matters.

items.title: $150 Million, 1,000 Fellows: Inside Anthropic’s Plan to Bring AI to Nonprofits - inc.com
items.summary: Anthropic, the company behind the Claude AI assistant, has launched a $150 million programme to help 1,000 fellows bring AI tools into the nonprofit and social sector. The initiative focuses on training people to apply AI to real-world community and public-benefit work.
items.why_it_matters: It offers a useful model for how structured fellowships and funding can rapidly build practical AI skills within mission-driven, public-good organisations like Tamkeen.

items.title: Why the US is Freezing Anthropic’s New Claude Models - Cyber Magazine
items.summary: Reports indicate the US government has placed restrictions on access to Anthropic's newest Claude models, effectively pausing their availability in certain contexts. The move highlights how national authorities can influence who can use specific AI systems.
items.why_it_matters: It is a reminder that access to leading AI tools can be controlled by their home governments, which is relevant when planning long-term reliance on foreign AI services.

items.title: Trump promised to bring order to AI oversight. That lasted 2 weeks. - Politico
items.summary: An article reviews how the US administration's early promises to create clear, consistent AI oversight quickly ran into difficulties and confusion. It illustrates how unsettled AI regulation remains even in major markets.
items.why_it_matters: Understanding the instability of AI rules elsewhere helps Tamkeen appreciate the value of clear, steady national governance frameworks at home.

items.title: Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced a method called Deployment Simulation, which tests how an AI model is likely to behave before it is released to the public. It uses real conversation patterns to spot problems early and improve safety checks.
items.why_it_matters: Better pre-release testing supports more trustworthy AI tools, which is important for any institution considering AI in public services and training.

items.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
items.summary: The UK government has partnered with Google DeepMind to build an AI prototype designed to speed up housing planning decisions. It is an example of a government using AI to make a slow, paperwork-heavy public process faster.
items.why_it_matters: It shows a practical, real-world case of AI improving government services, offering ideas for how public-sector workflows could be modernised.

items.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
items.summary: At the G7 summit, leaders including France's President Macron and India's Prime Minister Modi voiced concern that the US could cut off access to American AI systems at any time. A recent disruption to Anthropic's tools made this worry feel concrete for many governments.
items.why_it_matters: This directly affects national AI strategy in the Gulf, strengthening the case for sovereign AI capacity and reducing over-dependence on any single foreign provider.

items.text: ● DRAFT — for Policy & Strategy review
AI Pulse — Weekly AI News Digest
Thursday, 18 June 2026

body.text: This week's digest highlights a recurring theme: who controls access to AI, and what that means for governments and institutions that depend on it. We look at fresh efforts to bring AI skills to nonprofits and improve safety testing, alongside growing global concerns about reliance on US-based AI systems. For Tamkeen, these stories underline why building local capacity and clear governance matters.

items.text: [1. $150 Million, 1,000 Fellows: Inside Anthropic’s Plan to Bring AI to Nonprofits - inc.com](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitgFBVV95cUxONDk4cVozZFJfTThnQTBMNHZlWlcwM0VYME1GdTQ4R2NCbFB4TGZBMk12dG9VMHVDVUJuWGN6VW94WmxsSGVhMGVPaTE5UGNZRjBQY29hdzh4MWh0SkFPMW0zTGEyWHd3SDVlN0M1UldHWnFPWWoxX2R1NHM0QXREX28xVUdraWlvUGdoQUR1WTRMMGNjT1ZRMjN2WHNhcDlDTHBSRkdIVHZpVFlrUEVoTVAyR29KQQ?oc=5)

Anthropic, the company behind the Claude AI assistant, has launched a $150 million programme to help 1,000 fellows bring AI tools into the nonprofit and social sector. The initiative focuses on training people to apply AI to real-world community and public-benefit work.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It offers a useful model for how structured fellowships and funding can rapidly build practical AI skills within mission-driven, public-good organisations like Tamkeen.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 15 Jun 2026

[2. Why the US is Freezing Anthropic’s New Claude Models - Cyber Magazine](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxNa01Xck5MaThVVDA5X0paNDNycE5BS1QxTHFkb0h3WUVsSTB4azJQLV9xMVpxTi1RUEZFMU9iSHdlS19GbHpabVAxblNweXowRDVmVzJaR0ZtcEVKaWRFVTI1Ny1vdmdUdGhCWHIwRjJySk00MmVyNFoxMzRES3hyNVQwbkpiNXVkQ1E?oc=5)

Reports indicate the US government has placed restrictions on access to Anthropic's newest Claude models, effectively pausing their availability in certain contexts. The move highlights how national authorities can influence who can use specific AI systems.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It is a reminder that access to leading AI tools can be controlled by their home governments, which is relevant when planning long-term reliance on foreign AI services.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 17 Jun 2026

[3. Trump promised to bring order to AI oversight. That lasted 2 weeks. - Politico](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijAFBVV95cUxNcjJUeExDLVB0aHpfODdhNDBkY0o4Q01MQVhXLXpZMVRKdTc3SHAtV0FJUlFGVGNUaUV1bjZFNnFucHR6VzhNSmVKZzM5bUZVYlZHV1FWRmVBVkFiVzFTeVJ5eFRGRmxFWXVHcm1ENlVybUJNeWpRSnBVVmNfSDgxLVNPV1lRajhvcnY1bA?oc=5)

An article reviews how the US administration's early promises to create clear, consistent AI oversight quickly ran into difficulties and confusion. It illustrates how unsettled AI regulation remains even in major markets.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Understanding the instability of AI rules elsewhere helps Tamkeen appreciate the value of clear, steady national governance frameworks at home.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 16 Jun 2026
[4. Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment](https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation)

OpenAI has introduced a method called Deployment Simulation, which tests how an AI model is likely to behave before it is released to the public. It uses real conversation patterns to spot problems early and improve safety checks.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Better pre-release testing supports more trustworthy AI tools, which is important for any institution considering AI in public services and training.
OpenAI · 16 Jun 2026
[5. Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning](https://deepmind.google/blog/unlocking-uk-house-building-with-ai-accelerated-planning/)

The UK government has partnered with Google DeepMind to build an AI prototype designed to speed up housing planning decisions. It is an example of a government using AI to make a slow, paperwork-heavy public process faster.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It shows a practical, real-world case of AI improving government services, offering ideas for how public-sector workflows could be modernised.
Google DeepMind · 17 Jun 2026

[6. World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/17/world-leaders-want-american-ai-they-just-dont-want-america-to-be-able-to-turn-it-off/)

At the G7 summit, leaders including France's President Macron and India's Prime Minister Modi voiced concern that the US could cut off access to American AI systems at any time. A recent disruption to Anthropic's tools made this worry feel concrete for many governments.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** This directly affects national AI strategy in the Gulf, strengthening the case for sovereign AI capacity and reducing over-dependence on any single foreign provider.
TechCrunch — AI · 17 Jun 2026
Automated digest curated for Tamkeen by Policy & Strategy.

*** DRAFT — for Policy & Strategy review ***

AI Pulse — Weekly AI News Digest
Thursday, 18 June 2026
============================================================
This week's digest highlights a recurring theme: who controls access to AI, and what that means for governments and institutions that depend on it. We look at fresh efforts to bring AI skills to nonprofits and improve safety testing, alongside growing global concerns about reliance on US-based AI systems. For Tamkeen, these stories underline why building local capacity and clear governance matters.

1. $150 Million, 1,000 Fellows: Inside Anthropic’s Plan to Bring AI to Nonprofits - inc.com
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitgFBVV95cUxONDk4cVozZFJfTThnQTBMNHZlWlcwM0VYME1GdTQ4R2NCbFB4TGZBMk12dG9VMHVDVUJuWGN6VW94WmxsSGVhMGVPaTE5UGNZRjBQY29hdzh4MWh0SkFPMW0zTGEyWHd3SDVlN0M1UldHWnFPWWoxX2R1NHM0QXREX28xVUdraWlvUGdoQUR1WTRMMGNjT1ZRMjN2WHNhcDlDTHBSRkdIVHZpVFlrUEVoTVAyR29KQQ?oc=5
   Anthropic, the company behind the Claude AI assistant, has launched a $150 million programme to help 1,000 fellows bring AI tools into the nonprofit and social sector. The initiative focuses on training people to apply AI to real-world community and public-benefit work.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: It offers a useful model for how structured fellowships and funding can rapidly build practical AI skills within mission-driven, public-good organisations like Tamkeen.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 15 Jun 2026)

2. Why the US is Freezing Anthropic’s New Claude Models - Cyber Magazine
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxNa01Xck5MaThVVDA5X0paNDNycE5BS1QxTHFkb0h3WUVsSTB4azJQLV9xMVpxTi1RUEZFMU9iSHdlS19GbHpabVAxblNweXowRDVmVzJaR0ZtcEVKaWRFVTI1Ny1vdmdUdGhCWHIwRjJySk00MmVyNFoxMzRES3hyNVQwbkpiNXVkQ1E?oc=5
   Reports indicate the US government has placed restrictions on access to Anthropic's newest Claude models, effectively pausing their availability in certain contexts. The move highlights how national authorities can influence who can use specific AI systems.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: It is a reminder that access to leading AI tools can be controlled by their home governments, which is relevant when planning long-term reliance on foreign AI services.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 17 Jun 2026)

3. Trump promised to bring order to AI oversight. That lasted 2 weeks. - Politico
   https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijAFBVV95cUxNcjJUeExDLVB0aHpfODdhNDBkY0o4Q01MQVhXLXpZMVRKdTc3SHAtV0FJUlFGVGNUaUV1bjZFNnFucHR6VzhNSmVKZzM5bUZVYlZHV1FWRmVBVkFiVzFTeVJ5eFRGRmxFWXVHcm1ENlVybUJNeWpRSnBVVmNfSDgxLVNPV1lRajhvcnY1bA?oc=5
   An article reviews how the US administration's early promises to create clear, consistent AI oversight quickly ran into difficulties and confusion. It illustrates how unsettled AI regulation remains even in major markets.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Understanding the instability of AI rules elsewhere helps Tamkeen appreciate the value of clear, steady national governance frameworks at home.
   (Anthropic (via Google News) · 16 Jun 2026)

4. Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment
   https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation
   OpenAI has introduced a method called Deployment Simulation, which tests how an AI model is likely to behave before it is released to the public. It uses real conversation patterns to spot problems early and improve safety checks.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: Better pre-release testing supports more trustworthy AI tools, which is important for any institution considering AI in public services and training.
   (OpenAI · 16 Jun 2026)

5. Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
   https://deepmind.google/blog/unlocking-uk-house-building-with-ai-accelerated-planning/
   The UK government has partnered with Google DeepMind to build an AI prototype designed to speed up housing planning decisions. It is an example of a government using AI to make a slow, paperwork-heavy public process faster.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: It shows a practical, real-world case of AI improving government services, offering ideas for how public-sector workflows could be modernised.
   (Google DeepMind · 17 Jun 2026)

6. World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
   https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/17/world-leaders-want-american-ai-they-just-dont-want-america-to-be-able-to-turn-it-off/
   At the G7 summit, leaders including France's President Macron and India's Prime Minister Modi voiced concern that the US could cut off access to American AI systems at any time. A recent disruption to Anthropic's tools made this worry feel concrete for many governments.
   Why it matters for Tamkeen: This directly affects national AI strategy in the Gulf, strengthening the case for sovereign AI capacity and reducing over-dependence on any single foreign provider.
   (TechCrunch — AI · 17 Jun 2026)

------------------------------------------------------------
Automated digest curated for Tamkeen by Policy & Strategy.

Anthropic, the company behind the Claude AI assistant, has launched a $150 million programme to help 1,000 fellows bring AI tools into the nonprofit and social sector. The initiative focuses on training people to apply AI to real-world community and public-benefit work.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It offers a useful model for how structured fellowships and funding can rapidly build practical AI skills within mission-driven, public-good organisations like Tamkeen.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 15 Jun 2026

[2. Why the US is Freezing Anthropic’s New Claude Models - Cyber Magazine](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiigFBVV95cUxNa01Xck5MaThVVDA5X0paNDNycE5BS1QxTHFkb0h3WUVsSTB4azJQLV9xMVpxTi1RUEZFMU9iSHdlS19GbHpabVAxblNweXowRDVmVzJaR0ZtcEVKaWRFVTI1Ny1vdmdUdGhCWHIwRjJySk00MmVyNFoxMzRES3hyNVQwbkpiNXVkQ1E?oc=5)

Reports indicate the US government has placed restrictions on access to Anthropic's newest Claude models, effectively pausing their availability in certain contexts. The move highlights how national authorities can influence who can use specific AI systems.

**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It is a reminder that access to leading AI tools can be controlled by their home governments, which is relevant when planning long-term reliance on foreign AI services.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 17 Jun 2026

[3. Trump promised to bring order to AI oversight. That lasted 2 weeks. - Politico](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMijAFBVV95cUxNcjJUeExDLVB0aHpfODdhNDBkY0o4Q01MQVhXLXpZMVRKdTc3SHAtV0FJUlFGVGNUaUV1bjZFNnFucHR6VzhNSmVKZzM5bUZVYlZHV1FWRmVBVkFiVzFTeVJ5eFRGRmxFWXVHcm1ENlVybUJNeWpRSnBVVmNfSDgxLVNPV1lRajhvcnY1bA?oc=5)

An article reviews how the US administration's early promises to create clear, consistent AI oversight quickly ran into difficulties and confusion. It illustrates how unsettled AI regulation remains even in major markets.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Understanding the instability of AI rules elsewhere helps Tamkeen appreciate the value of clear, steady national governance frameworks at home.
Anthropic (via Google News) · 16 Jun 2026
[4. Predicting model behavior before release by simulating deployment](https://openai.com/index/deployment-simulation)

OpenAI has introduced a method called Deployment Simulation, which tests how an AI model is likely to behave before it is released to the public. It uses real conversation patterns to spot problems early and improve safety checks.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** Better pre-release testing supports more trustworthy AI tools, which is important for any institution considering AI in public services and training.
OpenAI · 16 Jun 2026
[5. Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning](https://deepmind.google/blog/unlocking-uk-house-building-with-ai-accelerated-planning/)

The UK government has partnered with Google DeepMind to build an AI prototype designed to speed up housing planning decisions. It is an example of a government using AI to make a slow, paperwork-heavy public process faster.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** It shows a practical, real-world case of AI improving government services, offering ideas for how public-sector workflows could be modernised.
Google DeepMind · 17 Jun 2026

[6. World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/17/world-leaders-want-american-ai-they-just-dont-want-america-to-be-able-to-turn-it-off/)

At the G7 summit, leaders including France's President Macron and India's Prime Minister Modi voiced concern that the US could cut off access to American AI systems at any time. A recent disruption to Anthropic's tools made this worry feel concrete for many governments.
**Why it matters for Tamkeen:** This directly affects national AI strategy in the Gulf, strengthening the case for sovereign AI capacity and reducing over-dependence on any single foreign provider.
TechCrunch — AI · 17 Jun 2026
